title: Login form remains usable when mobile keyboard opens
description: Verify username/password inputs and Sign In button stay visible or reachable when software keyboard is shown.
preconditions: App is opened on mobile device or emulator.
steps: Tap Username input and type a value.
Tap Password input and type a value.
Observe view shift behavior while keyboard is open.
Tap Sign In without dismissing keyboard.
expected: Focused input is not hidden by keyboard.
Sign In button remains tappable (or reachable after small scroll).
No clipped or overlapped controls.

title: Password show/hide control is touch-friendly
description: Validate touch target and behavior of the Show/Hide password toggle.
steps: Tap Password field and type text.
Tap Show control once.
Tap Hide control once.
expected: Password text visibility toggles correctly.
Control can be activated reliably with a thumb tap.
No accidental focus loss or input reset.

title: Pull-to-refresh updates student list on mobile
description: Verify native pull gesture triggers refresh indicator and list reload.
preconditions: User is authenticated and sees students list.
steps: Swipe down from top of list to trigger pull-to-refresh.
Wait for refresh indicator to stop.
expected: Refresh spinner appears during pull refresh.
List data is re-fetched without app freeze.
No duplicate rows introduced after refresh.

title: Student card actions are easy to tap on small screens
description: Validate Edit/Delete buttons on each card are not cramped or overlapping.
preconditions: User is authenticated and sees at least one student card.
steps: Scroll to a middle card in the list.
Tap Edit once.
Close modal.
Tap Delete once and cancel confirmation.
expected: Each action responds to a single tap.
Touch areas do not overlap.
Card layout remains readable without truncating key info.

title: Add Student modal handles keyboard overlap
description: Verify modal inputs and action buttons remain usable when keyboard is open in create flow.
preconditions: User is authenticated on students screen.
steps: Tap Add Student.
Focus First Name, Last Name, and Ratings fields one by one.
Type valid values.
Submit while keyboard is visible.
expected: Focused field remains visible above keyboard.
Submit and Cancel controls remain reachable.
Create action succeeds without layout jump glitches.

title: Unsaved changes prompt works in modal with touch actions
description: Validate Cancel vs Discard paths in the unsaved-changes confirmation on mobile.
preconditions: User is in Add Student modal with at least one edited field.
steps: Tap modal close or Cancel action.
In confirmation dialog, tap Cancel and verify modal remains.
Tap close again.
Tap Discard in confirmation dialog.
expected: First Cancel keeps modal open with entered values.
Discard closes modal and clears form state.
No accidental data loss without explicit Discard action.

title: Search and sort controls remain accessible at 320dp width
description: Verify search input and sort button layout for very small mobile widths.
preconditions: Viewport width is set to 320dp (or smallest available).
steps: Type query text in Search input.
Tap Sort button to cycle modes at least twice.
expected: Search input remains editable and visible.
Sort button remains fully visible and tappable.
No horizontal clipping or overlap between controls.

title: Delete confirmation dialog is readable and actionable on phone
description: Validate confirmation prompt content and action buttons on small mobile screens.
preconditions: User is authenticated with at least one student record.
steps: Tap Delete on a student card.
Observe dialog content and action buttons.
Tap Cancel.
Open dialog again and tap Delete.
expected: Dialog text is not truncated.
Buttons are clearly labeled and separated.
Cancel keeps data unchanged; Delete removes target student.

title: Orientation change preserves interactive state
description: Verify UI remains stable when rotating portrait to landscape and back.
preconditions: User is authenticated on students screen.
steps: Rotate device to landscape.
Use Search and open Add Student modal.
Rotate back to portrait.
expected: App does not crash or reset unexpectedly.
Current form/list state remains consistent.
Controls remain reachable after orientation changes.

title: Tap reliability under quick repeated interactions
description: Ensure repeated taps on refresh/add/logout do not create duplicate actions or locked UI.
preconditions: User is authenticated on students screen.
steps: Double-tap Refresh quickly.
Double-tap Add Student and close modal.
Tap Logout once while app is idle.
expected: Refresh runs once or serially without UI corruption.
Only one add modal is presented at a time.
Logout cleanly returns to login screen.
